title: In most countries, hourly earnings are higher in urban than rural areas
subtitle: Share of hourly earnings of urban population above rural population[footnote: A score above 0 means urban pay is higher, below 0 means rural pay is higher.]
source: International Labour Organization. (2025). SDG Labour Market Indicators (ILOSDG), EAR_4HRL_SEX_GEO_CUR_NB_A.
visdescription: This is a beeswarm chart with each dot representing a country. The x-axis shows how much higher or lower are urban wages compared to rural wages.
textParagraph: In 66 of the 79 countries for which we have data (80 percent), average hourly wages are higher in urban areas than in rural areas. For example, in Angola and Peru, average hourly wages in urban areas are 40 percent higher than rural areas.[reference: paydata]

scenes.text: In most economies, [emphasis: hourly earnings are higher in urban areas] than in rural areas.
Focusing on countries where [emphasis: urban workers earn more] than rural workers.
In a smaller set of countries, [emphasis: rural hourly earnings exceed urban].
